name: AI Agent Consumption Rules
description: Deterministic rules for AI agents to consume, interpret, and render Inkblot Studio components. No guessing. Every rule has one correct interpretation.

cliAndTui.rule: All terminal, TUI, and AI-prompt CLI experiences must follow system/cli.json — color roles, prompts, transcript layout, tool panels, NO_COLOR, and quality bar.

tokenResolution.rule: Always resolve tokens through the semantic layer. Never use primitive tokens directly in component code.
tokenResolution.exception: Primitive tokens are acceptable only when creating new semantic mappings or in token definition files.

colorMode.rule: All components must work in both light and dark mode. Use semantic tokens which auto-switch.
colorMode.implementation: Apply [data-theme='dark'] to <html> or <body>. Override CSS variables for dark values.

spacing.rule: Use spacing tokens from the 4pt grid. Never use arbitrary pixel values.

componentRendering.description: Step-by-step instructions for AI agents assembling UI components

validation: if loading: disable click handler, show spinner
if disabled: set aria-disabled, not disabled attribute (keeps in tab order)

input.type: text | email | password | number | tel | url | search
input.label: string (required)
input.name: string (required)

input.output:
{error ?

{error}

: null}{helpText && !error ?

{helpText}

: null}
validation: label is always required — never rely on placeholder alone
error message replaces help text when present
aria-describedby points to error or help text element

card.output:
<{interactive ? 'a href="{href}"' : 'div'} class='card card-{variant}' {interactive ? '' : ''}>{image ? : null}

{title}

{description ?

{description}

: null}{metadata ? {metadata} : null}
{actions ?
{actions.map(a =>
: null}
validation: interactive cards must be wrapped in <a> or have role='link'
truncate description to 3 lines, title to 2 lines

modal.output:
{title}

{children}
{footer ?
{footer}
: null}
validation: onClose is required — user must always be able to dismiss
return focus to trigger on close
message must be concise — 1-2 sentences max

layoutAssembly.description: How AI agents should assemble page layouts
steps: 1. Determine page type: content page, dashboard, form, listing, detail, auth
2. Select layout pattern from system/grid.json: singleColumn, sidebarLayout, cardGrid, splitScreen, masonry
3. Apply container: standard (1200px) for most pages, content (768px) for text-heavy, wide (1440px) for dashboards
4. Place components on grid using span rules from breakpoint table
5. Apply spacing: var(--inkblot-spacing-8) between major sections, var(--inkblot-spacing-4) between related items
6. Add page-level elements: header (sticky, z-index: sticky), main content, footer
7. Ensure color mode support: test with both light and dark tokens
8. Validate accessibility: contrast ratios, focus order, screen reader flow

main.content: cardGrid for metrics (span 3 each), table below for data

dashboardPage.header: sticky, z-index: sticky, contains breadcrumb + page title + actions

articlePage.content: heading-1 title, body-large intro, body-default paragraphs, images (16:9)

authPage.left: Brand illustration or gradient, centered logo
authPage.right: Form: heading-2, inputs, primary button, link to alternative auth

missingData.rule: Never show undefined, null, NaN, or empty strings in the UI.
missingData.strings: Show em-dash (—) for missing text values
missingData.numbers: Show em-dash (—) for missing numeric values

longContent.rule: Truncate with ellipsis. Provide full content via tooltip or expand.

overflowingContainers.rule: Content must never break layout. Use overflow-hidden on containers.
overflowingContainers.horizontal: overflow-x: hidden on page. Scroll on specific containers (tables, carousels).
overflowingContainers.vertical: Modals, dropdowns: max-height with overflow-y: auto.

dynamicContent.rule: All containers must handle variable content lengths without breaking.
dynamicContent.minHeight: Set min-height on cards, list items to prevent collapse when empty.
dynamicContent.maxHeight: Set max-height + overflow on expandable content to prevent page jump.

errorRecovery.networkError: Show inline error with retry button. Preserve user input.
errorRecovery.validationError: Highlight invalid fields. Scroll to first error. Announce via aria-live.
errorRecovery.notFound: Show 404 page with search bar and navigation links.
errorRecovery.unauthorized: Redirect to login. Preserve intended destination for post-auth redirect.

accessibility.automated: Run axe-core on every component. Zero violations for critical and serious.
accessibility.manual: Tab through every page. Verify focus order. Test with screen reader.
accessibility.contrast: Verify all text meets 7:1 (AAA). Use Chrome DevTools contrast checker.

visual.tool: Chromatic or Percy for visual regression
visual.coverage: Screenshot every component in: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, error, loading, dark mode

responsive.rule: No horizontal scroll at any breakpoint. No overlapping elements.
